market_status: CLOSED — Juneteenth Federal Holiday (NYSE/NASDAQ closed June 19)

macro_calendar_today.event: Juneteenth National Independence Day — US Market Holiday
macro_calendar_today.note: NYSE and NASDAQ closed. No RTH session. No 0DTE trading possible. Heartbeat tasks should no-op on holiday detection.

news_top_5.headline: Trump signs preliminary deal to end US-Iran war; crude oil drops to 3.5-month low
news_top_5.summary: President Trump signed a preliminary peace agreement Wednesday night, ending the US-Iran military conflict. Crude oil fell to a 3.5-month low on the news, easing inflation expectations and sparking broad risk-on sentiment in equities.

news_top_5.headline: S&P 500 closes +1.08% on Thursday June 18; ES futures +1.15% — Iran deal relief rally
news_top_5.summary: Thursday session saw broad risk-on buying after the Iran deal was announced Wednesday night. SPX closed up 1.08% heading into the Juneteenth holiday long weekend.

news_top_5.headline: Largest OPEX in history: ~$8.3T US options expire June 18 (moved from June 19 for Juneteenth)
news_top_5.summary: Monthly options expiration (3rd-Friday OPEX) moved to Thursday June 18 because June 19 is a federal holiday. ~$8.3T of options exposure — largest in history — cleared. Post-OPEX gamma reset increases directional sensitivity at Monday's open.

news_top_5.headline: VIX flat at 17.1-17.2 through Thursday session; post-FOMC volatility absorbed without fresh catalyst
news_top_5.summary: VIX held the 17-17.2 range through Thursday's choppy 4-point intraday range, indicating markets digested the June FOMC decision without a fresh fear catalyst. Holiday weekend expected to keep futures quiet.

news_top_5.headline: SPY June 18: choppy 743.35-747.00 range, ribbon mixed (12c spread), OPEX pin effect
news_top_5.summary: Per EOD digest, SPY oscillated in a ~4-point intraday range with ribbon spread ~12c (below 20c directional threshold). OPEX pinning effect contributed to chop — that effect clears with the expiry rolloff. Rule break: missing stop leg in only trade taken (-$24 on 4 contracts SPY 746C).

risk_regime_call.reasoning: Iran peace deal + oil at 3.5-month low removes the dominant macro tail-risk from prior weeks. SPX closed +1.08% Thursday with positive ES overnight. VIX flat at 17 (not fear-spiking) supports modest risk-on carry into Monday open. Post-OPEX gamma reset is the primary wildcard — cleared dealers no longer suppressing volatility, so Monday's first move could be exaggerated.

overnight_session_summary.note: Market closed today (Juneteenth). Holiday-weekend futures activity will be thin. Monday open is the next live session. Prior session close: SPY ~751-752 area based on swarm battle level 752.09.

scout_addendum_to_swarm.scout_caveat_to_swarm: Swarm output is from June 18 (yesterday's morning). CRITICAL GAP: Swarm did NOT flag that June 19 is Juneteenth — market is closed today; no session to trade. Additionally, swarm noted 'Monthly options expiry (3rd Friday June) creates pinning risk' but understated magnitude: $8.3T cleared (largest OPEX in history) — Monday open is a gamma-reset environment with elevated directional sensitivity. Swarm battle level 752.09 remains the key reclaim/reject pivot for Monday Premarket. Risk-on regime (Iran deal) is the scout addendum the swarm lacked.

today_no_trade_windows.reason: Juneteenth Federal Holiday — NYSE/NASDAQ closed all day. No 0DTE trading possible. No RTH session.

monday_preview.key_watch: Post-OPEX + post-Iran-deal setup entering Monday. $8.3T gamma cleared — market will be more directionality-sensitive Monday morning. Battle level 752.09 is the key reclaim/reject pivot (from swarm). Risk-on regime bias entering Monday but chart confirmation required before any trade.
risks: Post-OPEX realized vol spike — gamma-sold dealers no longer suppressing moves
Any Iran deal deterioration over 3-day holiday weekend (speculative — needs evidence by Monday morning)
Light Monday volume (post-3-day weekend) can exaggerate opening moves in either direction

scout_one_line_summary: MARKET CLOSED (Juneteenth); risk_on backdrop Iran-deal+SPX+1%; Mon opens post-$8.3T OPEX into higher vol sensitivity; battle 752.09
